title: Regulatory Reforms Key to Boosting Nigeria’s Investment Climate - FinancialEDGE
description: The Capital Market Correspondent Association of Nigeria (CAMCAN) held its 2025 Annual Conference in Lagos, focusing on "Regulatory Reforms: ISA 2025 and Nigeria's Investment Climate". The event brought together industry experts, including Moses Igbrude, National Coordinator of Independent Shareholders Association of Nigeria (ISAN), Chiamaka Ugo-Obidike, Team Lead Media Committee, Institute of Capital Market Registrars (ICMR),
html:
The Capital Market Correspondent Association of Nigeria (CAMCAN) held its 2025 Annual Conference in Lagos, focusing on “Regulatory Reforms: ISA 2025 and Nigeria’s Investment Climate”.

The event brought together industry experts, including Moses Igbrude, National Coordinator of Independent Shareholders Association of Nigeria (ISAN), Chiamaka Ugo-Obidike, Team Lead Media Committee, Institute of Capital Market Registrars (ICMR), and Odume Festus, Editorial/Project Director, Financial Edge.

Discussions centered on the need for regulatory reforms to enhance Nigeria’s investment climate.

Speakers emphasized the importance of creating a conducive environment for investors, highlighting the role of the Investment and Securities Act (ISA) 2025 in driving economic growth.

The conference provided a platform for stakeholders to share insights and recommendations for improving Nigeria’s capital market.

 

Photo Caption: L-R: Moses Igbrude, National Cordinator Independent Shareholders Association Of Nigeria (ISAN) Chiamaka Ugo-Obidike, Team Lead Media Committee, Institute of Capital Market Registrars (ICMR) and Odume Festus, Editorial / Project Director Financial Edge at the 2025 Annual Conference of Capital Market Correspondent Association Of Nigeria (CAMCAN) in Lagos.
